
[[Prosoche]] is paying attention to how you pay attention. Paying attention to the meaning you attach to events<ref>https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=LRF1fyfgYeM</ref>

People fuse the meaning and event together

It is the [[greater booster shot]] against [[foolishness]]

{{Quote|
Prosoche is ‘to pay attention.’ Meaning: pay attention to how you’re paying attention (your M-A-R). Learn to distinguish between your modal meaning and the event. (Vervaeke argues that this is the core of all of our modern cognitive psychotherapies. Learning to distinguish the meaning between the event and the meaning you give the event)|Mark Mulvey<ref name="mark" >https://web.archive.org/web/20210614061354/https://markmulvey.medium.com/awakening-from-the-meaning-crisis-by-john-vervaeke-ep-3dd53ea3703e</ref>}}

{{Quote|If the meaning and the event are fused, then you are con-fused|Mark Mulvey<ref name="mark" />}}

==In Videos==
[[Missed Party Video]] covers it

==See Also==

* [[Procheiron]]

== References ==

[[Category:John Vervaeke]]
[[Category:Git synced pages]]
[[sn:Meta-attention]][[Category:Foolishness]]
[[Category:Greater booster shots]]
[[Category:Attention]]
[[Category:Stoicism]]
[[Category:Meta-attention]]
[[sn:Meta-attention]][[Category:Ninefold vaccine]]
[[sn:Meta-attention]]
[[Category:Pages]]
